definitions.term: del presente Convenio de- Préstamo. Sección 7.02 = Restricciones en relación como los subprestamos
definitions.definition: a) El saldo del principal de los préstamos hechos por el deudor en o después de la fecha de la primera entrega de .acuerdo a los términos del presente Con­venio de Préstamo, deberá ser siempre por lo menos igual al total sin pagar o balance del préstamo. A mas de esto, el, deudor deberá realizar su programa conformidad con los contratos, reglas, regulaciones, política, acuerdos y arreglos sometidos o aprobados por el DLF, y el deudor deberá obtener la aprobación del DLF antes de cualquier modificación de -estos contratos, acuerdos, reglas, regu­laciones, política o negociaciones. b) El deudor podrá cargar un interés 'máxima del 6% anual en los subprestamos . c) El principal y el interés en los pagos indicados en los términos de los subprestamos no podrá variar por razón de cambios en el valor del colon costarri­cense. d) Los sub -prestamos podrán ser efec­tuados exclusivamente a personas u otras entidades con entradas netas, después de deducidos los impuestos, del equivalente cinco mil dólares estadounidenses ($ 5.000.00) o menos. e) No podrán hacerse sub -prestamos en los cuales la recuperación se presuma se obtendrá en menos de dos años. f) No podrán hacerse sub -prestamos o series de sub -prestamos relacionados en­tre sí a usuarios o grupos afiliados de usuarios por montos de cien mil dólares ($ 100,000.00) o mas de fondos del préstamo, o su equivalente, en moneda nacio­nal, que sean financiados en total o en parte con fondos del préstamo, a menos que tales sub -prestamos o series de ellos, sean aprobados por adelantado y en forma escrita por el DLF. Sección 7.03 = Información y rotulación: El deudor deberá hacer los arreglos ne­cesarios para asegurar el cumplimiento con tales instrucciones suministradas por el DLF, en relación con la publicidad para el préstamo, signos y rótulos en los lugares de actividades parcial o totalmente financiadas dentro de este Convenio de Préstamo, signos y rótulos de los artículos aceptables y sus embalajes, o de cualquier combinación entre ellos. Sección 7.04 = Mantenimiento de contabilidad y archivos; inspecciones: a) El deudor deberá mantener, o hacer que sean mantenidas, contabilidad y ar­chivos, para identificar las empresas y artículos aceptables financiados por me­dio de sub -prestamos, para establecer su costo y su uso, y para indicar el progreso de los proyectos financiados par­cial o totalmente por los su prestamos. Asimismo el deudor deberá mantener contabilidad y archivos de acuerdo con los requisitos técnicos de contabilidad, que muestren su estado financiero y cual­quier cambio en su estructura de capital, activo y pasivo, ganancias y pérdidas, v que sean sometidos a auditorajes regu­larmente, a los intervalos que el DLF sugiera, por auditores aceptables por el DLF y el deudor. Tales contabilidades y archivos deberán mantenerse hasta que todas las sumas debidas al DLF bajo este Convenio de Crédito hayan sido pagadas en su totalidad. b) El DLF tendrá el derecho de, en cualquier ocasión razonable, examinar ta­les contabilidades y archivos, así como los documentos, correspondencia, memorándums , etc., en relación con este Con­venio de Crédito. El deudor y el fiador autorizaran al DLF para inspeccionar las operaciones del deudor en Costa Rica y la utilización de todos los artículos acep­tables financiados por los sub -prestamos. El deudor y el fiador deberán cooperar, y darán asistencia razonable, para que el DLF pueda realizar tales exámenes, observaciones y revisiones sin ningún tro­piezo, y pagara o hará los arreglos nece­sarios para el pago, en todas las oportu­nidades, en que representantes autoriza­dos del DLF quieran visitar algunas zonas del país, para propósitos relacionados con el préstamo, c) El deudor y el fiador deberán sumi­nistrar adecuadamente al DLF la información financiera y de cualquier otra clase, relacionada con el préstamo y con las actividades financiadas por los sub­prestamo s, cuando el DLF razonablemente lo solicite. Sección 7.05 = Comisiones, tarifas y otros pagos: a)El deudor consiente en que deberá informar rápidamente al DLF sobre toda comisión, tarifa o pago de cualquier clase, adicional a aquellos comprendidos en la Sección 4.01 del que han sido hechos o se ha acordado hacer a cualquier per­sona, firma o compañía (que no sea el pago regular de los empleados de tiempo completo del deudor) como retribución por servicios de buena fe profesionales, técnicos u otros comparables, para la preparación o presentación de la solicitud que ha llevado a la obtención del crédito por parte del DLF, o en conexión con cualquiera de las negociaciones para tal fin. En el caso de que el DLF considere cualquier comisión, tarifa o pago que se ha reportado bajo esta Sección o bajo la Sección 4.01, como inadecuada, el deudor, por la presente, está de acuerdo en efec­tuar los ajustes a la misma, satisfactorios para el DLF, e informar al beneficiario de tal comisión, tarifa o pago de esta condición. b) El deudor y el fiador garantizan que ninguna comisión, tarifa o pago de cual­quier clase, salvo las comisiones, tarifas o pagos reportados al DLF en relación con la Sección 4.01 del y la Sección 7.05 (a) ha sido o será pagada a cualquier persona, firma o compañía (que no sea pago regular de los empleados de tiempo completo del deudor o del fiador), en relación con la preparación y presentación de la solicitud que ha promovido la obtención del préstamo del DLF o en relación con cualquiera etapa de las negocia­ciones para tal objeto. Sección 7.06 = Notificación de desarrollos adversos: El deudor deberá informar al DLF, a la mayor brevedad, sobre cual­quier condición que interfiera, o pueda interferir, con el servicio del préstamo o de los pagares, o con el desarrollo general del programa. Artículo VIII Garantías Sección 8.01 = Garantía de las obligaciones del deudor : Sin limitaciones o res­tricciones, en relación con las demás dis­posiciones contenidas en el presente Con­venio de Préstamo, por medio de la pre­sente el fiador garantiza, absoluta e in­condicionalmente, conjunta y separada­mente como deudor solidario junto con -el deudor, el cumplimiento, de acuerdo con los términos del presente Convenio de Préstamo y los pagares, del venci­miento y puntual pago del principal v los intereses del préstamo y de los pagares emitidos y subsistentes a que se refiere el artículo III, o de cualquier otro .pago requerido dentro del Convenio de Préstamo o los pagares. Sección 8.02 - Garantía en los pagares: Con respecto a cualquier pagare emitido por el deudor en relación con la Sección o en cambio o reposición de los pagares emitidos, el fiador, por medio de la presente, consiente en endosar tales documentos con su garantía incondicional de pago, en la forma indicada en el texto especificado del Anexo III que acompañan al presente Convenio de Préstamo. Sección 8.03 = Deberes posteriores del fiador: El fiador deberá cooperar de lleno con el DLF para asegurar que los propósitos del presente Convenio de Préstamo se cumplan. Para tal fin, el fiador deberá suministrar la información, y tomara las medidas necesarias para hacer operable la garantía de los pagares, en la forma en que el DLF razonablemente solicite. Sección 8.04 = Impuestos sobre el Convenio de Préstamo, pagares y pagos de los mismos: El fiador consiente en que el presente Convenio de Préstamo y los pagares del mismo estarán libres de todo impuesto, y de que el principal y los inte­reses de los mismos serán pagados sin deducción alguna por concepto de impues­tos, tarifas y otros cargos que determi­nen las leyes en Costa Rica. Sección 8.05 Tipo de moneda para los pagos: a) El fiador consiente en que cualquier tipo de moneda usado para los pa­gos al DLF de acuerdo a este Convenio, o de los pagares del mismo, podrá ser usado por el DLF o por cualquier otra agenda del Gobierno de los Estados Uni­dos de Norte América, para cualquier gasto o pago del DLF o de cualquier otra agenda del Gobierno de Estados Unidos, incluyendo cualquier gasto o pago del DLF para efecto de préstamos u otras transacciones autorizadas por la legislación que gobierna al DLF, en vigencia en el momento del uso de tal moneda, estipulando, - sin embargo, que el uso de tal moneda de pago por el DLF o cual­quier otra agencia del Gobierno de los Estados Unidos para la adquisición de productos o servicios para exportar de Costa Rica o sus territorios, o la venta de tal moneda de pago por otras divisas, a entidades que no sean el Gobierno de los Estados Unidos será realizada única­mente bajo aceptación previa del fiador ; previendo además, que el fiador, al estu­diar las posibilidades de tales usos, clara especial atención a las proposiciones del DLF para usar tal moneda de pago para financiar la exportación de productos o servicios de Costa Rica o sus territorios que se usaran en conexión con proyec­tos financiados por el DLF en terceros países. El DLF consiente en que tomara en consideración la posición económica de Costa Rica para el uso posible de tal moneda de pago, recibidas por el DLF de acuerdo a los términos del presente Convenio de Préstamo o sus pagares. b) Cuando el DLF determine que se ha operado una mejora substancial en la po­sición económica y financiera de Costa Rica v en sus perspectivas económicas y financieras a largo plazo, que permitan una expansión del uso por el DLF de la moneda de pago, estando esta determinación basada en la existencia de una o más de las condiciones siguientes: i) Mejoramiento significativo en la situación económica y financiera interna del país; ii ) Tendencias favorables en la balanza de pagos y existencia de reservas de di-visas en Costa Rica; y iii ) Posibilidad de Costa Rica para efectuar pagos futuros al DLF en distin­ta moneda que la llamada moneda de pago, sin que esto interfiera en el ser­vicio de las deudas con cualquiera otra agencia del Gobierno de los Estados Unidos, con prestamistas privados o con cualquier organización internacional de cual los Estados Unidos forme parte ; el fiador afirma que llevara a cabo negociaciones, a requerimiento del DEE, para el futuro convenio destinado a cam­biar tales sumas por otras divisas, inclu­yendo dólares estadounidenses, o para el uso de ellas para financiar la exportación de productos o servicios de Costa Rica o sus territorios en relación con proyectos financiados por el DLF en terceros países, por hasta la totalidad de la moneda reci­bida por el DLF bajo términos del pre­sente Convenio de Préstamo o de sus pagares. c) El fiador y el DLF, reconociendo la deseabilidad de que el uso de la moneda de pago obtenida por el DLF como pago de intereses o de capital en, otros préstamos hechos en Costa Rica rinda el máxima efecto, ya sea antes o después de la formalización del presente Conve­nio de Préstamo, acuerdan, consultarse de tiempo en tiempo, a requerimiento de uno de ellos, sobre tales tenencias de cir­culante y su uso, incluyendo la trans­ferencia de ellas a otras divisas que pu­diera ser dólares estadounidenses, en una base de mutuo acuerdo. ARTICULO IX Soluciones del DLF Sección 9.01 = Causas de Rescisión: Cese de entrega del préstamo: En caso de ocurrir alguna de las siguientes circunstancias, se considerara como una causa de rescisión del Convenio, que dará derecho al DLF, a su criterio, para terminar la entrega de fondos compren­dida dentro del Convenio de Préstamo: a) La no realización del pago completo de cualquier pago parcial del principal, o del pago de intereses a su vencimiento, o de cualquier otro pago previsto en el presente Convenio de Préstamo o en los pagares, previendo que el DLF podrá, si tal Cosa ocurriere, ejercer por decisión suya, sus derechos, únicamente mientras tal falta de pago se mantenga; b) El incumplimiento, par parte del deudor, de cualquiera de las estipulacio­nes o disposiciones contenidas en el pre­sente Convenio de Préstamo. c) Cualquier otro incumplimiento en que incurra el deudor o el fiador en relación a cualquier otro Convenio con el DLF. d) La determinación de que cualquier representación o garantía, hechas por o en representación del deudor o del fiador, para obtener el préstamo o en relación con el Convenio de Préstamo es inco­rrecta, en el aspecto material e) Una situación extraordinaria, que a juicio del DLF, haga imposible que el deudor o fiador sean capaces de cumplir sus obligaciones contraídas en el pre­sente Convenio, o que el préstamo pueda llenar los propósitos previstos por la legislación que gobierna al DLF. Sección 9.02 = Aceleración del préstamo: Cuando se presente alguna de las causas de rescisión a que se refieren los incisos a), b), c) y d) de la Sección 9.01, y de acuerdo con los términos de tales incisos, el DEL podrá, a su criterio, declarar, la totalidad o parte del principal del préstamo sin pagar y los pagares, como vencidos, e inmediatamente cobrables, especificando cuales cuotas del mismo están vencidas, y podrá establecer que el principal y los intereses que se declaran vencidos e inmediatamente cobrables lo serán en dólares estadounidenses; mediante tal declaración, el principal, las cuotas y todos los intereses devengados se con­sideraran vencidos y serán cobrables in­mediatamente. Sin embargo, el DLF no ejecutara la facultad indicada, en esta Sección, a menos que la causa de rescisión no sea corregida dentro de un período de sesenta días, a partir de la no­tificación del DLF de que intenta ejer­cer la acción a que se refiere esta Sección. Sección 9.03 = Renuncia del rescindimiento : La tardanza en ejercer su de­recho, o el no ejercerlo, o el que no tome medidas el DLF, de acuerdo con los términos del Convenio de Préstamo o de los pagares, como resultado de alguna de las cansas de rescisión especificadas en la Sección 9.01, no podrá ser considerado como aprobación tacita o renuncia de los derechos del DLF, creados como resultado de que se haya presentado tal condición o cualquiera otra semejante. Sección 9.04 = Reembolsos: Si el DLF considera que cualquier desembolso por el realizado, dentro de las condiciones del presente Convenio, no está respal­dada por adecuada documentación suministrada por el deudor de acuerdo con los términos de este Convenio de Préstamo o no ha sido hecho o usado de acuerdo con los términos del mismo o viola la legislación que gobierna al DLF, el deudor deberá pagar al DLF, dentro de un periodo de treinta días, a partir de la respectiva notificación, la suma en dólares estadounidenses, que .no exceda a desembolso, previendo, sin embargo, que tal notificación sea hecha por el DLF a más tardar cinco altos después de la fecha en que el desembolso fue efectuado. El DLF reducirá el préstamo en la suma correspondiente a tal pago, después de recibirlo y adecuara, en consecuencia, los pagos que el deudor deberá realizar a los pagares. ARTICULO X Misceláneas Sección 10.01 = Fecha efectiva del Convenio de Préstamo: El presente con­venio deberá entrar en vigor en el día y fecha que se especifico. Sección 10.02 = Nombramiento y Facultades de los Representantes: a) Toda acción llevada a cabo bajo el presente Convenio de Prestamos por el Deudor, Fiador o el DLF puede ser des­arrollada por sus representantes, debidamente autorizados, y todos los derechos del deudor o de DLF bajo el presente Acuerdo pueden ser ejercidos por los respectivos representantes autorizados. b) El deudor y el fiador deberán de­signar un individuo o individuos por nombre y titulo y otorgarle a él o a ellos, autoridad para designarlos representan­tes del Deudor y del fiador para sus ne­gociaciones con el DLF, de acuerdo con la Sub -sección anteriormente mencio­nada. Dichos representantes deberán te­ner autoridad para solicitar entregas bajo el presente Convenio y para suscribir cualquier documento necesario durante el Convenio en nombre del Deudor o Fiador, excluyendo los pagares. Hasta que el DLF no haya recibido la notificación escrita de la revocación de nombramiento de un representante, el DLF aceptara la firma de ese representante en cualquier documento como evidencia ple­na de que la acción efectuada por dicho representante está autorizada por el Deu­dor o Fiador. Sección 10.03 = Sucesores a los Derechos de DLF: Si por cualquier ley de los Estados Unidos, o por alguna asignación, una Agencia del Gobierno de los Estados Unidos, o una corporación, se hace cargo de los derechos y obligaciones de DLF bajo este Convenio de Prestamos y Paga­res, dicha Agencia representara al DLF para los fines del presente Convenio, de los pagares y del use de la "moneda de pago". Sección 10.04 = "Entregas": De acuerdo con este Convenio, el termino de "en­tregas" querrá decir cualquier desem­bolso del DLF, ya sea directo o indirecto, al Deudor o su representante. Sección 10.05 = "Secciones", "Artículos": Toda referencia, en este Convenio de Prestamos, "Secciones o Artículos", deberá ser considerada como referente a las Secciones y Artículos del Convenio de Préstamo, a menos que se especifique en otra forma. Sección 10.06 = Ley Aplicable: Este Con­venio de Préstamo y cada uno de los Pa­gares deberán ser considerados coma contratos efectivos bajo la ley del Dis­trito de Columbia, de los Estados Unidos de América, y deberá ser efectuado y estará regido por las Leyes del Distrito de Columbia, Estados Unidos de América. Sección 10.07 = Notificaciones: Cual­quier notificación, requerimiento o comunicación dada, hecha o enviada por el Deudor, el Fiador o el DLF, en rela­ción con este Convenio de Préstamo a los pagares, deberá ser por escrito, y se considerara legalmente dada, hecha o enviada a la parte a quien está dirigida cuando se entregue, ya sea a mano, por correo, por telegrama, por cable o por radiograma al destinatario, en su dirección particular, de acuerdo a la siguiente lista: Para el Deudor: Dirección Aérea: Banco Nacional de Costa Rica San José, Costa. Washington, D.C . En caso de que estas direcciones fue­sen cambiadas, se hará la notificación de ello a las otras partes contratantes. En fe de lo cual el Deudor, el Fiador y DLF, cada uno actuando Por medio de sus respectivos representantes debida­mente autorizados, firman el presente Convenio de Préstamo, que regirá a par­tir del día y ano indicados en el mismo. Por Banco Nacional de Costa Rica Por Republica de Costa Rica Por Development Loan Fund DLF Loan N° 205 COSTA RICA: RURAL AGRICULTURAL DEVELOPMENT LOAN AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE BANCO NACIONAL DE COSTA RICA, THE REPUBLIC OF COSTA RICA AND THE DEVELOPMENT LOAN FUND Dated: September 21, 1961 LOAN AGREEMENT Agreement, dated the 21 st day of September, 1961, between the Banco Nacional de Costa Rica, an autonomous financial institution of the Republic of Costa Rica (hereinafter called the "Borrower"), the Republic of Costa Rica (hereinafter called the "Guarantor") and the Development Loan Fund (hereinafter called the "DLF"), an agency of the Government of the United States of America . WITNESSETH Whereas the Borrower has undertaken to make medium and long term loans to finance activities needed for agricultura production and processing in Costa Rica; and Whereas the Borrower has made application for a loan from the DLF to be used to assist in financing this program and Whereas the Guarantor "is prepared to guarantee unconditionally and as primary obligor, payment of all obligations of the borrower incurred pursuant to such loan and to give certain other assurances relating thereto; and Whereas the establishment of such a loan will assist, on the basis of self help and mutual cooperation, the development of the economic resources and productive capabilities of Costa Rica; Now, therefore, the borrower, the Guarantor. and the DLF hereby agree follows: ARTICLE I The Loan, The Program, Use of Loan Section 1.01 = The Loan: The DLF agrees to lend to the borrower, through disbursements by the DLF in accordance with this Loang Agreement, on the terms and conditions in this Loan Agreement an amount not to exceed five million U nited States dollars ($ 5,000,000), or its equivalent in other currencies, for the program described in Section 1.02. The amount so disbursed shall hereinafter be called the ·'Loan·'. Section 1 . 02 = The Program: This loan is made to assist the borrower to carry out a program (hereinafter called the "Program") of making medium and long ­term loans to finance the capital requi­rements, other than land rental or pur­chase, necessary to initiate, sustain and improve agricultural production and pro­cessing in categories of activities mu­tually agreeable to the borrower and the DLF. Section 1.03 - Use of Loan, Eligible Items: The borrower shall use the loan, in accordance with the term of this Loan Agreement, exclusively to make sub-loans (hereinafter called "Sub-Loans") to fi ­nance the reasonable local currency and foreign exchange costs of equipment, ma­terials and services, in categories of activity mutually agreeable to the borrower and the DLF, required to accomplish the objectives of the program (hereinafter called "Eligible ltems "). ARTICLE II Terms of Repayment, Interest Section 2.01 = Payment Obligations: The borrower agrees to repay the loan, and to pay interest on the loan, in accordance with the terms of this Loan Agreement and the promissory notes (hereinafter called "Note" or "Notes'') issued and out ­standing pursuant to Article III . All pay­ments required of the borrower under this Loan Agreement, except for pay­ments pursuant to Sections 3.04 and 9.04, with respect to portions of the loan against "which Notes habe been issued in accordance with Article III , shall be made to the holder or holders of such Notes, and shall be applied first to the payment of accrued interest on the Notes and the Notes and then to the repayment of prin­cipal of the Notes due and payable. All other payments, including payments pur­sant to Sections 3.04 and 9.04 , shall be made to the DLF and shall be applied first to the payment of accrued interest and then to the repayment of principal due unless the section governing such payment provides otherwise. The person or entity entitled to payment as specified in this Section shall hereinafter be called the "Payee". Section 2.02: Currency of Repayment: All obligations of the borrower to make payments under this Loan Agreement and the Notes shall be computed and stated in United States dollars, and except for dollar prepayments required under Sec ­tion 2.08 and dollar payments required under Section 9.02 and 9.04, shall be dis­charged by the payment of such coin, currency as at the time of payment is legal tender in Costa Rica for payment of public and private debts (hereinafter called "Currency of Repayment"). Section 2.03 = Amount of Currency of Repayment: (a) The amount of Currency of Repayment equivalent to the United States dollar amount of the obligation for which payment is due shall be com ­puted on the basis of the rate of exchange prescribed in Section 2.04 existing on the "Reference Date", as defined in Section 2.05, applicable to such payment; provided, that in any case where payment is made after the due date for that pay­ment, the Payee may require that it be computed on the basis of the prescribed rate of exchange existing on the date of payment. (b) In the event that there is no pres­cribed rate of exchange on the due date for a payment or during the preceding thirty (30) days, the amount of Currency of Repayment equivalent to the United States dollar amount of the obligation for which payment is due shall be computed and paid on the basis of the prescribed rate of exchange existing on the date, nearest preceding the due date for the payment, on which such a rate can be ascertained. Within sixty (60) days after the first date after such due date on which a prescribed rate of exchange can be ascertained, the borrower shall, upon the request of the Payee, make prompt payment to the Payee of the omount of Currency of Repayment re­quired to make the total payment of Currency of Repayment equivalent in value to the United States dollar amount of the obligation against which the payment was made, computed on the basis of the prescribed rate of exchange existing on the first date following the Referente Date on which such a rate can be ascertained. Section 2.04 = Rate of Exchange: For purposes of the payments required by this Article, the prescribed rate of exchange between Currency of Repayment and United States dollars on any particular date shall be the effective rate exchange at which United States dollar are soId or offered for sale on that date in exchange for Currency of Repayment to residents of Costa Rica, exclusive of Government entities, for effecting: (1) the payment of interest and repayment of principal on loans; (2) the transfer of dividends and other forms of earnings on capital investments in Costa Rica. and (3) the transfer of investment capital; provided, that there is only one such rate in Costa Rica for such transactions. If there is no such single rate of exchange applicable to all of the three categories of transactions referred to in the pre­ceding sentence, the applicable rate of exchange on any particular date shall be the highest ( i . e., the largest number of units of Currency of Repayment per United States dollar) effective rate of exchange at which United States dollars are sold or offered for sale on that date to residents of Costa Rica, exclusive of government entities, in exchange for Cur­rency of Repayment to effect transactions within any of the three categories re­ferred to in the preceding sentence. Section 2.05 = Reference Date: With respect to any payment required of the borrower under this Loan Agreement and the Notes, the Payee may at any time give notice to the borrower of the United States dollar amount of the obligation for which payment is due, and may de­signate in such notice a date, in no event to be more than thirty (30) days prior to the date payment is due, which shall be termed the "Reference Date", lf no date is thus designated prior to the date on which such payment is due, the Re­ference Date shall be deemed to be the date on which the payment is due. Section 2.06 = Interest: Interest shall accrue, from the dates of the respective disbursements under this Loan Agreement, at the rate of three and one-half percent (3 ½%) per annum, computed on the basis of a 365-day year. Interest shall be payable semi-annually on all outstand­ing balances until the loan is repaid, the first interest payment to be due and payable on a date no later than six (6) months after the first disbursement to be specified by the DLF. Section 2.07 = Amortization: The loan shall be repayable in semi-annual instal­Iments in accordance with the amortiza­tion schedule set forth in Schedule 1, attached to this Loan Agreement, the first such installment to be repayable six (6) months after the first interest pay­ment is due. lf at any time it is deter­mined that the full amount authorized to be loaned will not be drawn, or if, upon final disbursement by the DLF under this Loan, Agreement, the aggregate amount of disbursements by the DLF does not equal the amount authorized to be loaned, the amount authorized to be loaned shall be reduced and, at the discretion of DLF, the amortization schedule may be revised by reducing installments not then due so that those installments shall be in such amounts as shall provide for approximately equal aggregate amounts of princi­pal and interest; provided, however, that there shall be no reduction of instal­lments then due or against which repay­ment has been made or Notes have been issued by the borrower and negotiated by the DLF. Section 2.08 = Prepayment: (a) With respect to any part of the loan for which Notes have not been issued and negotiated, the borrower shall have the right to prepay in Currency of Repayment or in United States dollars, without penalty, on the due dates of installments of prin­cipal, all or any part of the princípal of such part of the loan by payment of the principal amount so prepaid with interest thereon to the date prepayment is made: provided, that the borrower may not make prepayments in Currency of Re­payment ( i ) prior to the first installment due date following final disbursement of the loan, (ii) when there is no rate of exchange as prescribed by Section 2.04 on the due date prepayment is made, or (iii) except as the DLF may otherwise agree in writing, after the occurence of anv of the Events of Default referred to in Section 9.01. Any prepayment shall be credited to the remaining installments of principal in the inverse order of their maturity, and the United States dollar value of prepayments in Currency of Repayment shall be determined on the basis of the prescribed rate of exchange exist­ing on the Reference Date applicable to the installment due elate on which the prepayment is made. (b) If the prescribed rate of exchange existing on the date one year after the date a prepayment in Currency of Repayment was made would provide a greater number of units of such currency per United States dollar than the rate of exchange at which the credit for the pre­payment was computed, the DLF shall adjust such credit on the basis of such later rate of exchange. ARTICLE III Promissory Notes Section 3.01 = Issuance of Notes: If and as the DLF shall from to time to time request, the borrower shall, as soon as practicable and within such period, not less than thirty (30) days after the date of any request therefor , as the DLF shall specify in such request, execute and de­liver to or on the order of the DLF Notes in the aggregate principal amount spe­cified in such request, not exceeding, however the aggregate amount of the loan which shall be outstanding at the time of such request and for which Notes shall not theretofore have be en so de­livered or requested: Section 3.02 = Terms of Notes: The No­tes shall bear interest at the same rate as the loan, and shall have such matu­rities and be in such denominations as the DLF shall specify in such request except that the aggregate principal amount of such Notes of any maturity shall not exceed the amount of the co­rresponding installment of the loan. Section 3.03 = Form of Notes: The No­tes shall be printed, lithographed or en­graved, shall be written in the English language, and shall substantially conform in text to the Serial Note Form set forth in Schedule Il attached to this Loan Agreement. Section 3.04 = DLF Guaranty of Notes: If the DLF shall transfer ,. assign or sell any Note and shall extend any guaranty in connection with part or all of any payment thereunder , the borrower shall reimburse the DLF for any amount paid by the DLF under such guaranty by reason of any failure of the borrower to make payment in accordance with the terms of such Note and this Loan Agree­ment. Section 3.05 = Rights of Holders of Notes: No holder (other than the DLF) of any Note shall, by virtue of being the holder therefore, be entitled to exercise any rights under this Loan Agreement or be subject to any of the conditions or obligations imposed upon the DLF hereby. The provisions of this Section shall not impair or affect any rights or obligations under the terms of any Note. Section 3.06 = Payments on Notes: The payment of the principal of any Note shall pro tanto discharge the obligation of the borrower to repay that part of the loan to which the Note relates; and the payment of ínterest on any Note shall pro tanto discharge the obligation of the borrower to pay interest on that part of the loan to which the Note re­lates. Section 3.07 = Negotiability of Notes : Each Note issued pursuant to this Loan. Agreement shall be freely negotiable by the holder thereof. Section 3.08: Duties of Borrower with respect to Notes : The borrower shall promytly furnish to the DLF such in­formation and legal opinions and take such other steps, including, but not limited to, the preparation and execution of such applications and other documents, modification of the Notes to per­fect negotiability, the appointment of a trustee to protect Note holders, and the maintenance of an agency for authentication of such Notes or for making payment thereunder ", as the DLF shall request in order to facilitate the public or private sale of the Notes or to list any of the Notes on any securities exchange. Section 3.09: Exchange of Notes: At any time when the holder of any Note or Notes shall so request, the borrower shall, upon surrender of such Note or Notes for such purpose, execute and deliver to the holder a new Note or Notes in exchange therefor in an aggregate principal amount equal to the unpaid principal amount of the Note or Notes surrendered, and of like tenor. bearing interest from the date to which interest, has been paid on such Note or Notes and payable to the order of such person or persons and in such other denomination or denominations as such holder may reasonably request. Section 3.10 = Replacement of Notes: Upon receipt of evidence satisfactory the borrower of the loss, theft, destruction or mutilation of any Note, and in the case of loss, theft or destruction upon receipt of indemnity reasonably satisfactory to the borrower, or, in the case of any such mutilation, upon surrender and cancellation of such Note, the borrower shall execute and deliver a new Note of like tenor and unpaid principal amount in lieu of such lost, stolen, destroyed mutilated Note. Section 3.11 = Transfers of Notes: The DLF shall notify the borrower and the guarantor of any transfer by it of the Notes and shall record on the Notes so transferred all payments made thereon prior to such transfer. ARTICLE IV Conditions Precedent Section 4.01 = Conditions Precedent to initial Financing: Prior to and as conditions precedent to the first disbursement under this Loan Agreement, the borrower, except as the DLF may other­wise agree in writing, stall furnish to the DLF: (a) An opinion or opinions of the Pro­curador General of Costa Rica , or of other legal counsel satisfactory to the DLF, demonstrating to the satisfac­tion of the DLF: ( i ) That the Loan Agreement has been duly authorized or ratified by, and executed and delivered on behalf of the borrower and the guarantor and constitutes a valid and binding obligation of the borrower and the guarantor in accordance with its terms; (ii) That the Notes, if and when executed and delivered in ac­cordance with the Loan Agree­ment, will constitute valid and binding obligations of the bo­rrower in accordance with their terms and that, except as stated in such opinion, no further sig­nature nor formality is required for that purpose; and (iii) That the guaranties by the gua­rantor of payment of the Notes when executed and delivered in accordance with this Loan Agreement will constituye valid and binding obligations of the guarantor in accordance with their terms and that, except as stated in such opinion, no fur­ther signature nor formality is required for that purpose; and (iv) That the individual or individuals designated pursuant to Section 4.01 (b) has or have the autho­rity to carry out any of the acts required or permitted in accor­dance with Section 10.02 (b); (b) Designations of an individual or individuals by name or title in accor­dance with Section 10.02 (b); (c) A report from the borrower con­taining the amounts and recipients of any commissions, fees or payments of any kind which have been made or agreed to be made to any person, firm or corporation (other than re­gular compensation to ful -time offi­cers and employees of the borrower or the guarantor) as compensation for bona fide professional, technical or other comparable services in con­nection with preparing or presenting the application which has resulted in the making of the loan by the DLF or in connection with any negotia­tions incident to obtaining the loan; (d) Evidence satisfactory to the DLF that the foreing exchange and local currency required for the Program in addition to this loan will be avail­able as necessary; (e) A list mutually agreeable to the bo­rrower and the DLF of the catego­ries of activity eligible for financing by the loan; (f) A Letter of Information setting forth the basic terms, rules regulations, policies and procedures to be used in making. Sub-Loans and such other representations and warranties; con­cerning the status, operations, affi­liations and assets of the borrower as the DLF shall reasonably request. Section 4.02 = Terminal Date for Full fillment of Conditions Precedent: If the borrower has not completed the steps required in Section 4.01 by November 15, 1961, or such other date as shall be agreed to in writing by the DLF, the DLF may ay any time thereafter at its option ter­minate this Loan Agreement by giving notice to the borrower and the guarantor. ARTICLE V Letters of Commnitment and Disbursement Section 5.01 = Disbursement: When all of the Conditions Precedent to disbur­sement under the Loan Agreement have been met, the DLF will make disburse­ments to the borrower or to its designee to reimburse the borrower for Sub-Loans extended by it. Such disbursements shall be made in minimum amounts of one hundred thou­sand United States dollars ($100,000) or its equivalent in other currencies, and shall be supported by such documenta­tion as the DLF may from time to time specify, but in any case the borrower may receive a minimum of one disbur­sement a month. Section 5.02 : Other Forms of Disbursement: Disbursement may also be made under the Loan Agreement: by such other means and such other procedures as the borrower and the DLF may mutually agree to in writing. Section 5.03 = Costs Paid in other Cu­rrencies: Where a cost under this Loan Agreement is payable and has been paid in a currency other than United States dollars, the DLF at its option will make disbursements in such currency or in United States dollars. Where disbur­sement is made in a currency other than United States dollars, the DLF will charge the loan with the amount of United States dollars paid by the DLF for the for the appropriate amount of such other currency, or where the DLF holds such other currency, with the amount which the DLF would have had to pay on date of disbursement for the appropriate amount of such other currency. Section 5.04. = Terminal Date for Requests for Disbursement: Except as the DLF may otherwise agree in writing, no disbursement shall be made against documentation submitted after December 31, 1963. ARTICLE VI Covenants Concerning Procurement Section 6.01: Eligibility Date: No costs may be financed by Sub-Loans which arise out of orders or contracts firmely placed or entered into prior to the effective date of this Loan Agreement. Section 6.02 = Reasonable Price: The borrower covenants that it will include in its Sub-Loan Agreements a covenant providing that no more than reasonable prices shall be paid for items financed thereunder . Reasonable price shall normally approximate the lowest competitive price for the item procured, quality, time and cost of delivery, and other factors considered. Section 6.03 = Notification of Small Business: For all Sub-Loans the borrower shall furnish to the DLF soch information at such time and whith such promptness as the DLF may require whith respet to the Elígible Items to be procured thereunder so that small business firms in the United States shall have a reasonable opportunity to participate in supplying Eligible Items financed under this loan Agreement. Section 6.04 = Sources of Procurement: (a) AII Eligible Items financed by this loan shaIl have their origin in and shall be procured from countries included in Code 899 of the lnternational Cooperation Administration Geographic Code Book as in effect at the time Elegible Items are firmly ordered or otherwise contracted for. (b) Except as the DLF may otherwise agree in writing, Eligible Items (including transportation but excluding marine in surance ) financed by Sub - Loans or a series of related Sub-Loans to any borrowers under which fifty thousand United States dollars ($ 50,000) or more of the Loan may be disbursed, or its equivalent, shall be procured from the United States of America or Costa Rica. In such cases, except as the DLF may otherwise agree in writing, procurement, other than professional services, shall be based upon a reasonable selection of suppliers and no more than reasonable prices as defined in Section 6.02 shall be paid. Section 6.05 = Shipping: (a) No Eligible Items may be financed by Sub-Loans or procured under contracts financed by Sub- Loans which are shipped to Costa Rica by any transportation medium owned, operated, or under the control of a country (other than Costa Rica) not included in Code 899 of the lnternational Cooperation Administration Geographic Code Book as in effect at the time of shipment. (b) The borrower shall ensure that at least fifty percent (50%) of the gross tonnage of all equipment and materials (computed separately for dry bulk carriers, dry cargo liners and tankers) financed under this Loan Agreement which may be transported on ocean vessels shall be transported on privately owned United States-flag commercial vessels, to the extent such vessels are available at fair and reasonable rates for United States flag commercial vessels. Determinations that United States-flag commercial vessels are not so available must be approved by the DLF. (c) In cases arising under Section 6.04 (b), where Eligible Items are pro­cured in the United States, costs of shipment of such Eiligible Items, except as the DLF may otherwise agree in writing may be financed only if the Eligible Items were transported on United States flag carriers. Section 6.06 = Marine Insurance: Marine insurance may be financed under this Loan Agreement, provided that such insurance is placed at the lowest available competitive rate; provided further, however, that if in connection with the placement of marine insurance on shipments financed under United States legislation authorizing assistance to other nations, Costa Rica discriminates by statute, decree, rule or regulation against any marine insurance company authorized to do business in any States of the United States of America, marine insurance financed under this Loan Agreement shall during the continuance of such discrimination be placed in the United States with a company or companies authorized to do a marine insurance business in any State of the United States of America. ARTICLE VII Additional Covenants and Warranties Section 7.01 = General Covenant: Bo­rrower shall make the Sub-Loans with due diligence and efficiency in conformity with sound banking and financial practices, and under supervisión of qua­lified and experience management. The borrower shall include a covenant in all Sub-Loan Agreements to the effect that all equipment and machinery financed by the Sub- Loan shall be adequately main­tained and repaired. in accordance with sound engineering and mechanical prac­tices, as necessary or appropriate. The borrower shall exercise its rights in relation to the activities financed in whole or in part by this Loan so as to protect the interests of the borrower and the DLF. to meet the requirements and dis­charge the obligations of the borrower under this Loan Agreement and to facilitate the succes of such activities. Ex­cept as the DLF may agree in writing the borrower shall insure that the loan is utilized to finance equipment, mate­rials, and services in those categories of activities set forth on the list required by Section 4.01 (

definitions.term: of this Loan Agree­ment. Section 7.02 = Covenants Concerning Sub=Loans
definitions.definition: (a) The aggregate principal amount outstanding on Loans made by the Borrower on or after the date of the first disbursement under this Loan Agree­ment, always shall be at least equal to the total unpaid principal balance of this Loan. Further, the borrower shall cause the program to be carried out in con­formity with any contracts, rules, regu­lations, policies, agreements or arrange­ments submitted to or approved by the DLF, and the borrower shall obtain DLF concurrence prior to any material modi­fication of these contracts, agreements, rules, regulations, policies or arrange­ments. (b) The borrower shall charge a maximum of six percent (6%) interest per annum on Sub-Loans. (c) Principal and interest payments under the terms of the Sub-Loans. shall not vary in response to changes in the value of the Costa Rican Colon. (d) Sub-Loans shall be made exclusively to individuals or other entities with a net income after all taxes of the equivalent of five thousand United States dollars ($ 5000) or less. (e) No Sub-Loan shall be made unless it is reasonably expected to be repaid in two (2) years or more. (f) No Sub-Loan nore series of related Sub-Loans to any borrower or affiliated group of borrowers using one hundred thousand United States dollars ($ 100,000) of the loan or more, or its equivalent in Costa Rican colones , shall be financed in whole or part by this loan unless such Sub-Loans or series of related Sub-Loans are approved in advance in writing by the DLF. Section 7.03 - Information and Marking: The borrower shalI make appropriate arrangements to assure compliance with such instructions as may be furnished by the DLF with respect to publicity for the Loan, signs at the sites of activities financed in whole or in part under this Loan Agreement and marking of Eligible ltems and the shipping containers thereof, or any combination of the foregoing measures. Section 7.04 - Maintenance of Books and Records; Inspections: (a) The borrower shall maintain or cause to be maintain or cause to be maintained books and records adequate to identify the enterprises and Eligible ltems ­financed by Sub-Loans; to disclose their cost and their use; and to indicate the progress of the projects financed in whole or in part by Sub-Loans. The borrower shall also maintain books and records in accordance with sound accounting principIes and practices, showing its financial condition and any changes in capital structure, assets and liabilities, profits and losses, which shall be audited regularly, at such intervals as the D.L.F may specify, by auditors acceptable to the DLF and the borrower. Such books and records shall be maintained until all sums due under this Loan Agreement and the Notes have been paid in full. (b) The DLF shall have the right at reasonable times to examine such books and records and all other documents corresponde , memoranda and other records relating to this Loan Agreement. The borrower and the guarantor shall enable the DLF to inspect the borrower's operations in Costa Rica and the utilization of all Eligible Items financed by Sub-Loans. The borrower and the guarantor shall cooperate with, and give reasonable assistance to, the DLF to enable such examination, observation and review to be accomplished fully and ex­peditiously and shall afford, or arrange to have afforded, all reasonable oppor­tunity for authorized representatives of the DLF to visit any part of Costa Rica for purposes related to the loan. (c) The borrower and guarantor shall promptly furnish to the DLF such finan­cial and other reports and information relating to the loan and the activities financed by the Sub-Loans, as tre DLF may reasonably request. Section 7.05 = Commissions, Fees and other Payments: (a) The borrower co­venants that it wiII promptly report to the DLF any commisions , fees or pay­ments of any kind in addition to those reported pursuant to Section 4.01 (c) which are made or agreed to be made to any person, firm or corporation (other than regular compensation to full-time officers and employees of the borrower) as compensation for bona fide profes­sional technical or other comparable services in connection with preparing or presenting the application which has resulted in the making of the loan by the DLF, or in connection with any negotiations incident to obtaining the loan. In the event that the DLF shall deter­mine any commission, fee or payment reported under this section or under Section 4,01 (c) to be unreasonable, the borrower hereby agrees to effect an ad­justment thereof satisfactory to the DLF and to inform the recepient of any such commission, fee, or payment of this condition. (b) The borrower and the guarantor warrant that no commissions, fees or payments of any kind, other than the commissions, fees or payments reported to the DLF pursuant to Section 4.01 (c) and 7.05 (a) have been or will be made to any person, firm or corporation (other than regular compensation to full-time officers and employees of the borrower or the guarantor) in connection with preparing or presenting the application which has resulted in the making of the loan by the DLF or in connection with any negotiations incident to obtaining the loan. Section 7.06 - Notice of Adverse Developments: The borrower shaIl promptly inform the DLF of any conditions which interfere with, or thereaten to interfere with the servicing of the loan or Notes ­or the carrying out of the Program. ARTICLE VIII Guaranty Section 8.01 = Guaranty of Obligation of Borrower: Without limitation or restriction upon any of the other covenants on its part contained in this Loan Agreement, the guarantor hereby absolutely and unconditionally, jointly and severally as primary obligor with the borrower undertakes to make, in accordance with the terms of this Loan Agreement and the Notes, the due and punctual payment of the principal of and the interest on the Joan and the Notes issued and outstand ing pursuant to Article lII , or any other payment required under the Loan Agreement or the Notes. Section 8.02 - Guaranty on Notes: With respect to any Notes issued by the borrower pursuant to Section 3.01 or in exchange for or replacement of Notes, so issued, the guarantor hereby agrees to endorse thereon its, unconditional guaranty of payment in the form and text specified in Shedule III attached to this Loan Agreement. Section 8.03 = Further Duties or Guarantor: The guarantor shall cooperate fullv with the DLF to assure that the purposes of this Loan Agreement will be accomplished. To that end, the guarantor shall furnish such information and take such steps related to making the guaranty of the Notes operative as the DLF shall reasonably request. Section 8.04 = Taxation on Loan Agreement, Notes and Payments Thereunder : The guarantor agrees that this Loan Agreement and the Notes shall be free from, and the principal of and interest on the loan and the Notes shall be paid without deduction for and free from any taxation, including fees and impositions, imposed under the laws of Costa Rica. Section 8.05 . Use of Currency of Repayment: (a) The guarantor agrees that any Currency of Repayment paid to the DLF under this Loan Agreement or the Notes may be used by the DLF or by any agency of the Government of the United States for any expenditures of or payments by the DLF or any agency of the Government of the United States, including any expenditures of or payments by the DLF for purposes of loans or other transactions authorized by the legislation governing the DLF in effect at the time of use of the Currency; pro­vided, however, the use of such Currency of Repayment by the DLF or any agency of the Government of the United Stales to finance the purchase of goods and services for export from Costa Rica or its territories, or the sale of such Currency of Repayment for other currencies to entities other than the Government of the United States will be undertaken only upon agreement in advance by the guarantor: provided further, that the guarantor in considering proposals for such uses will give particular attention to proposals by the DLF to use such Currency of Repayment to finance ex­ports of goods or services from Costa Rica or its territories for use in con­nection with projects financed by the DLF in third countries, The DLF agrees that it wiII take into account the economic position of Costa Rica in any contemplated use of the Currency of Repay­ment received by the DLF under this Loan Agreement or the Notes; (b) When the DLF determines that there has been a substantial improvement in the economic and financial position of Costa Rica and in the long-run economic and financial prospects of Costa Rica which permit an expanded use by the DLF of Currency of Repayment. such determination being based on the exis­tence of one or more of the following: i ) Significant improvement in the in­ternal economic and financial situation of Costa Rica ; ii) Favorable trends in the balance of payments and foreign exchange holdings of Costa Rica : iii) Ability of Costa Rica to make fu­ture payments on DLF loans in curren­cies other than the Currency of Repay­ment without interfering with the ser­vice of debts owing to any united States Government agency, private lenders, or any international organization of which the United States is a member; The guarantor affirms that it will un­dertake to negotiate at the request of the DLF a further agreement to provide for the transfer into other currencies includ­ing United States dollars, or for the use to finance exports of goods or services from Costa Rica or its territories in co­nnection with projects financed by the DLF in third countries of any Currency of Repayment received subsequent to the aforesaid DLF determination under this Loan Agreement or the Notes; (c) The guarantor and the DLF, r­ecognizing the desirability of maximizing the effective use of Currency of Repay­ment held by the DLF as a result of interest and repayments of principal on other loans made in Costa Rica either before or after the signing of this Loan Agreement, agree to consult from time to time at the request of one or the other on such holdings and their use, including the transfer of such holdings into other currencies including United States dollars on a mutually agreeable basis. ARTICLE IX Remedies of the DLF Section 9.01 : Events of Default; Termination of Disbursements: The occurence of any of the following events shall be deemed to be an Event of Default, entitling the DLF, at it option to terminate disbursements under the Loans Agreement. a) Failure to make full payment of any installment of principal or payment of ment required under this Loan Agreement or the Notes; provided, that the DLF may on the basis of any such default, exercise any of its options under this Section only while such default continues: b) A breach on the part of the borrower in the performance of any other covenant or agreement under this Loan Agreement; c) A default shall have occured under any other agreement between the borrower or the guarantor and the DLF; d) Determination that any representation or warranty made by or on behalf of the borrower or the guarantor obtaining the loan or pursuant to this Loan Agreement is incorrect in a material respect; e) An extraordinary situation which blethe DLF determines makes it improbable that the borrower or the guarantor will be able to perform its obligations under this Loan Agreement or that the loan will fulfill the purposes of the legislation governing the DLF. Section 9.02 : Acceleration of Loan: Upon the occurrence of any of the Events of Default referred to in subsections (a), (b), (c), and (d) of Section 9.01, and in accordance with the terms of such subsections, the DLF, at its option, may declare all or any part of the unpaid principal of the loan and the Notes to be due and payable immediately specifying which installments are included, and may declare that such principal and installments declared to be due and payable shall be payable in United States dollars, and upon any such decla­ration such principal and installments and all interest accrued thereon shall become so due and payable immediately; provided, however, that the DLF shall not exercise its option under this Section unless the default is not cured within sixty (60) days alter the DLF gives notice of its intention to exercise its option under this Section. Section 9.03 = Waivers of Default: No delay in exercising or omission to exer­cise, any right, power or remedy accruing to the DLF under this Loan Agreement or the Notes arising as a result of the occurrence of any of the events specified in section 9.01 hereof shall be construed as an acquiescence by the DLF in such event or as a waiver by the DLF of its rights, powers, or remedies arising as a waiver by the DLF of its rights, powers, or remedies arising as a result of the occurrence of that event or of any other such event. Section 9.04 = Refunds: lf the DLF de­termines that any disbursement made by it under this Loan Agreement is not Supported by the documentation submit­ted by the borrower in accordance with this Loan Agreement or is not made or used in accordance with the terms of this Loan Agreement, or is in violation of the legislation governing the DLF, the borrower shall pay to the DLF, within thirty (30) days after receipt of request, an amount in United States dollars not to exceed the amount of such disburse­ment; provided, that such request by the DLF shall be made not later than five (5) years after the date on which the disbursement was made. The DLF shall reduce the loan by the amount of any such payment upon its receipt by the DLF, and the DLF shall cause the obli­gation of the borrower to make payments under the Notes to be reduced accord­ingly. ARTICLE X Miscellaneous Section 10.01 = Effective Date of Loan Agreement: This Loan Agreement shall enter into effect as of the day and year first above written. Section 10.02 = Use of Representatives: (a) All actions to be performed under the Loan Agreement by the borrowers, the guarantor or the DLF may be per­formed by their respective duly autho­rized representatives, and all rights of the borrower or the DLF under this Loan Agreement may be exercised by their respective duly authorized repre­sentatives. b) The borrower and the guarantor shall designate and individual or individuals by name or title and grant to him or them authority to designate the representatives, of the borrower and the guarantor in its dealings with the DLF in accordance with the above subsection Such representatives shall be given authority to request disbursements under this Loan Agreement and to execute any and all documents called for under this Loan Agreement on behalf of the borrower and the guarantor excluding the Notes. Until receipt by the DLF of written notice of revocation of anv designation made pursuant to this Section the DLF may accept the signature of such representatives on any instrument as conclusive evidence that any action effected by such instrument is authorized by the borrower or the guarantor. Section 10.03 = Successors, to Rights of DLF: If, by operation of any law of the United States or by virtue of assignment, any corporate or other agency of the Government of the United States succeeds to the rights and obligations of the DLF under this Loan Agreement and Notes, such shall be deemed to be the DLF for purposes of this Loan Agreement, the Notes and use of Currency of Repayment. Section 10.04 = "Disbursements": As used in this Loan Agreement, the term "disbursements" shall mean any payments by the DLF whether made directly to the borrower or its designee. Section 10.05 = "Sections", "Articles": All references in this Loan Agreement to "Sections" or "Articles" shall bedemed to refer to Sections and Articles of this Loan Agreement, unless specific provision is made to the contrary. Section 10.06 = Applicable Law: This Loan Agreement and each of the Notes shall be deemed to be a contract made under the laws of the District of Columbia, United States of América , and shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the District of Columbia, United States of America.
